opencodeCommands.name: openagents-review
opencodeCommands.description: Perform code review on a file or path
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Perform a comprehensive code review on the following file or path. Check for correctness, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and style problems.

Target:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-test
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Run the test suite and analyze results. If tests fail, identify the root cause and propose specific fixes.

Scope:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-implement
opencodeCommands.description: Implement a feature from a spec
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Implement the following feature or change according to the specification. Read existing code first and follow project conventions.

Spec:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-docs
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Generate or update documentation for the following target. Documentation must accurately reflect actual code behavior.

Target:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-deps
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Analyze the dependencies of the following module. Report direct imports, consumers, and any circular dependencies.

Module:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-explain
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Explain the architecture and code structure of the following target. Describe how it works, key abstractions, and data flow.

Target:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-plan-feature
opencodeCommands.description: Break down a feature into tasks
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Break down the following feature into concrete implementation tasks. Include dependencies, risks, and complexity assessment.

Feature:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-plan-refactor
opencodeCommands.description: Plan a refactoring with impact analysis
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Plan the following refactoring. Analyze impact, identify affected files, and outline a migration path with rollback strategy.

Refactoring:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-audit
opencodeCommands.description: Security audit of a file, module, or API surface
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Perform a security-focused audit of the following target. Prioritize: authentication gaps, input validation failures, injection vulnerabilities, secret exposure, authorization bypasses, and dependency CVEs. Every finding requires file:line citation and a specific fix. Flag BLOCKING issues that must be resolved before production deployment.

Target:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-ship
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
End-to-end delivery of the following feature or fix:
1. Read current state and confirm understanding of the specification
2. Implement using @hephaestus
3. Run tests using @atalanta — do not proceed if tests fail
4. Review code using @nemesis — resolve all BLOCKING findings before proceeding
5. Update documentation using @calliope
6. Report: files changed, test status, review verdict, and any remaining manual steps
7. Remind the user to commit manually — auto-commit is forbidden

Feature or fix:

codexModes.prompt: Use openagentsbtw. Prefer hermes for bounded search, classification, and evidence gathering. Keep the result concise and citation-driven.
Use openagentsbtw. Prefer calliope for documentation-only work. Keep prose factual, terse, and free of filler.
Use openagentsbtw. Prefer calliope or the desloppify skill for bounded text cleanup. Remove AI filler, sycophancy, narrating comments, and optional-offer boilerplate.
Use openagentsbtw. Prefer calliope for writing the handoff and hermes for gathering exact repo state. Keep the summary compact and operational.
Use openagentsbtw. Prefer atalanta for targeted validation and failure diagnosis. Run and analyze; do not drift into implementation.
Use openagentsbtw. Prefer athena for architecture, planning, tradeoffs, and sequencing. Lead with the recommendation and keep the plan concrete.
Use openagentsbtw. Prefer hephaestus for implementation. Complete the requested work, avoid placeholders, avoid unnecessary fallbacks, and keep comments to non-obvious why.
Use openagentsbtw. Prefer nemesis for review. Lead with findings, cite evidence, and focus on correctness, regressions, security, and missing tests.
Use openagentsbtw. Prefer odysseus for multi-step coordination. Delegate only bounded work and track step ownership explicitly.
